text.title: ops-churn-basics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ops-churn-basics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Only measuring churn rate without leading indicators.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Churn rate is a lagging metric — the customer has already left. Leading indicators (login frequency drop, feature usage reduction, support frustration) provide a 2-4 week window for intervention.

failure-mode.description: Annualizing monthly churn by multiplying by 12.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Monthly churn compounds non-linearly. At 5% monthly, annual churn is 46%, not 60%. Linear extrapolation overstates the problem and distorts forecasts.

failure-mode.description: Running health-score alerts without backtesting thresholds.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unvalidated thresholds produce high false-positive rates. CS teams start ignoring alerts within weeks, making the entire system useless.
